target: data/sections.json (part4.html) — surgical one-clause insertion in §4.2.7a "Even more constrained: constant-r orbital reading" subsection. Adds an inline parenthetical immediately after the "Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km" claim that cross-references the P1 robustness paragraph above (which treats the stricter R_disc/180 projection convention). Closes the c2 H4 "cross-convention tension" minor finding (queue_id 286). Existing R_disc/90 lead-in paragraph (added by 2026-05-01 decider run) already addressed half the convention dependency; this patch closes the remaining gap by giving the reader an explicit pointer to where the alternative convention is handled.
source_tracker_entry: baby-orphan-ISS-1747 (analyst-baby PROP-029 safety-net pickup; escalated to Opus on 2026-05-14T15:46:03Z with reason "Content requires substantive argument revision or physics verification beyond verification-class scope" — see review_class_rationale below for why I believe the escalation was over-cautious and this is in fact verification-class work).
review_class_rationale: Mechanical insertion of a single explanatory clause cross-referencing prose that already exists earlier in the same section. No new numerical claims, no new physics derivation, no verdict changes. The two numbers in the new clause (75 km Coimbra–San Pablo half-Δr, 82 km Ebro–Coimbra half-Δr) are quoted verbatim from the P1 "Robustness check (projection convention)" paragraph, which is itself the section that c2 audited and approved (the inserted clause is a back-reference, not an independent derivation). Curmudgeon at re-review only needs to confirm: (a) the parenthetical's numbers match P1, (b) the cross-reference text accurately summarises what P1 says, (c) the insertion didn't break surrounding sentence flow. Batchable with adjacent verification items per PROP-025; baby's escalation reason invoked "substantive argument revision" which would have been correct if I had to re-derive R_disc/180 geometry, but the existing P1 paragraph already did that work. PROP-025 author authority: declared verification.

patches.target_locator: part4.html, §4.2.7a "Even more constrained: constant-r orbital reading" subsection, second paragraph ("The above charity already grants the dome a non-circular Moon orbit…"), penultimate sentence ending "already outside an 85-km window." Insertion sits between that sentence and the bolded "Under the strict constant-r orbital reading…" sentence.
patches.current_text: Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km — already outside an 85-km window.
patches.proposed_text: Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km — already outside an 85-km window. (Under the stricter R<sub>disc</sub>/180 projection convention treated in the P1 robustness paragraph above, this pair’s Δr halves to ≈ 75 km, and Ebro–Coimbra to ≈ 82 km — both inside the 85-km band — which softens the constant-r reading to “max 2” rather than max 1; the 0/9 headline survives either convention.)
patches.rationale: c2 H4 (queue_id 286) flagged a cross-convention tension: the constant-r subsection cites Coimbra–San Pablo Δr=150 km without saying which AE convention that number is in, while P1 above cites Coimbra–San Pablo Δr≈75 km and Ebro–Coimbra ≈82 km under the stricter R_disc/180 convention. A reader who skims into this subsection without reading P1 first sees "150 km" as if it were the universal answer. The 2026-05-01 decider run added a lead-in clarifying "Under the standard R_disc/90 convention used in the per-station table above…" which fixes the implicit-convention half of the complaint; this patch closes the other half by pointing the reader at the location in the section where the alternative convention is handled. Numbers (75 km, 82 km, max 2) all quoted verbatim from the existing P1 "Robustness check (projection convention)" paragraph; no fresh computation. Locator verified unique in part4.html (single occurrence of the literal string).

kernel_of_truth: The dome's text has been adversarially reviewed multiple times; the careful reader the cross-reference is for is, in our context, the curmudgeon agent itself. There is a genuine sense in which adding parentheticals to a paragraph that is already structurally correct can clutter prose without changing the argument's reach. A defender of the unpatched text could reasonably say: "the R_disc/90 lead-in is enough, the reader will see P1 if they scroll up."
why_kernel_doesnt_save_dome: Not a dome claim — this is internal review polish, not an attack on the dome. The kernel-of-truth here is whether the parenthetical is worth the prose cost. Verdict: yes, because (a) the curmudgeon explicitly recommended it as the fix for c2 H4, (b) the cost is one sentence-clause, (c) the benefit is that a section-reader who lands on "Even more constrained" via in-page deep-link (the entire site is anchor-navigable) doesn't have to scroll up to discover which projection convention they're in. The kill itself is unaffected either way — 0/9 stands under both R_disc/90 and R_disc/180.

holes_addressed.summary: Cross-convention tension between 150 km (R_disc/90) in constant-r subsection and 75 km / 82 km (R_disc/180) in P1 above.
holes_addressed.resolution: Inline parenthetical added directly after the 150 km claim, cross-referencing P1 and quoting its 75 km / 82 km numbers to make the convention dependency explicit on the page where the 150 km appears.

new_evidence_added: None. All numbers in the inserted clause (75 km Δr Coimbra–San Pablo, 82 km Δr Ebro–Coimbra, "max 2" softening) are verbatim from the existing P1 paragraph, which itself is the c2-reviewed and approved text.

anticipated_objections.objection: The lead-in already says R_disc/90; the parenthetical is redundant.
anticipated_objections.response: The lead-in establishes the convention for the per-station table; the constant-r subsection then derives a separate "max 1" claim from a different orbital assumption (constant-r), and the reader needs to know whether the alternative convention changes that derivation. The parenthetical is the smallest possible answer: it spells out the change (max 1 → max 2) and points at where the alternative is handled. Without it the reader can't tell from the local prose whether "max 1" is robust to convention.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding a parenthetical clutters the punchline of the subsection (the bolded "max 1" sentence).
anticipated_objections.response: Granted — but the parenthetical sits BEFORE the bolded sentence, not inside it. The bolded sentence still lands clean. The cost is one sentence-clause of additional prose between the setup and the punchline.

preflight_verification.locator_uniqueness: Confirmed single occurrence of "Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km — already outside an 85-km window." in data/sections.json (part4.html). String.replace will replace the only instance.
preflight_verification.numerical_consistency: Inserted numbers (75 km, 82 km, max 2) match the P1 "Robustness check (projection convention)" paragraph verbatim (verified by re-reading P1 in current sections.json).
preflight_verification.sentence_flow: Insertion sits between two sentences ending in a period. The new clause is a self-contained parenthetical that does not break syntactic agreement with the surrounding text.
preflight_verification.html_entity_consistency: Uses the same entity vocabulary already present in surrounding text: <sub>...</sub> for subscripts,   for non-breaking spaces, ’/“/” for typographic quotes, ≈ as literal Unicode (matches existing usage of literal Unicode for ≈ / – / —).
preflight_verification.side_effects: No anchor IDs touched; no table values touched; no chart data touched. data/sections.json prose-only edit.

tldr_update_rationale: Section TLDR (ps-tldr) summarises the 0/9 kill result; the convention dependency is body-paragraph detail, not headline. Adding the parenthetical does not change the section's punchline or argumentative thrust.
newsworthy_rationale: Internal review polish, not a substantive finding.
follow_ups: ISS-1753 (SEC-4.2.7a EXP-276.c2 H1, minor) on the R_p ∝ h_Moon non-linear-correction footnote derivation also belongs to this c2 cluster and is similarly small. Reasonable to batch in a future analyst run.

ISS-1736 (SEC-4.2.7a c1 H1, moderate), ISS-1737 (SEC-4.2.7a c1 H7, moderate), and ISS-1738 (EXP-260 c1 H2-H4, moderate) are c1 findings against EXP-268; subsequent EXP-276 integration appears to have rewritten the affected paragraphs in ways that may already address them. Spot-check on a future run: if current text no longer contains the c1-flagged wording, propose OBE closure for those three. (Confirmed via grep this run that ISS-1736's exact flagged clause is no longer present in data/sections.json — its fix has effectively landed but the issue is still assigned-analyst because no closure event was emitted.)
summary_for_decider: Single-clause insertion in §4.2.7a closes c2 H4 (minor cross-convention tension). Numbers all quoted verbatim from already-reviewed P1 paragraph. Pre-flight passes (locator unique, numbers consistent, sentence flow preserved). review_class=verification per PROP-025 — batchable with adjacent verification items. After integration, close ISS-1747 fixed.
id_reservation_note: EXP-409 reserved; prior EXP-408 (baby-orphan-ISS-1521) was authored in the 2026-05-16T23:09Z analyst run and is on FUSE awaiting workspace-sync push.
